observable_oracle: Repository namespace, object format, worktree identity, loaded-root prefix, both binding epochs, pipeline, schema, and policy each separate the namespace digest, and canonical bytes round-trip exactly.
failure_risk: Distinct repositories, object formats, worktrees, root prefixes, authority epochs, or semantic pipeline versions could alias one mutable manifest namespace.

observable_oracle: A writer that observed a miss detects the intervening predecessor, refreshes it, and successfully retries the verified higher-authority delta merge.
failure_risk: A nil predecessor race could permanently strand valid pending manifest changes.

observable_oracle: A two-manifest quota evicts one of three namespaces, accounting stays within count and byte limits, per-manifest record overflow is rejected, and bounded maintenance completes.
failure_risk: Manifest cardinality, bytes, or record arrays could grow without bound or GC could cross namespace authority incorrectly.
notes: Slice 2A finite manifest quota and GC

observable_oracle: Replacing the root after secure removal but before return causes insecureDirectory; the detached deletion is never reported as successful against the replacement root.
failure_risk: A root swap after unlink could make removeNamespace return true for a deletion performed only in the obsolete tree.

observable_oracle: A new-layout store commits while an old-layout store is gated after lock replacement; the old store then fails before mutation and cannot overwrite the new domain.
failure_risk: Replacing an in-layout flock inode could split cross-process serialization and allow simultaneous old/new mutation domains.

observable_oracle: Replacing the root after read admission causes a miss after final layout, shard, and leaf authority validation; no detached-tree hit is returned.
failure_risk: A reader holding valid old descriptors could return a checksum-valid snapshot after the authoritative root pathname was replaced.

observable_oracle: Shard replacement at read, pre-write, quarantine, and post-rename boundaries yields miss or security failure; displaced shard descriptors never report successful mutation authority.
failure_risk: A valid descriptor for an unlinked shard could be read, quarantined, or published through after its pathname authority moved to another inode.
notes: Slice 2A shard pathname identity and commit fencing

observable_oracle: A gated generation-1 touch cannot overwrite generation-2 records or access; simulated interruption after temporary write preserves epoch 1 with one removable orphan, while interruption after rename exposes the complete epoch-100 access-only snapshot.
failure_risk: A delayed or interrupted advisory touch could roll back newer bindings, expose a partial snapshot, or leave unbounded temporary residue.
notes: Slice 2A stale and crash-safe access refresh

observable_oracle: A newer registered writer session publishes its authority; the older session is rejected even when it supplies that exact authority as predecessor, and the current snapshot remains intact.
failure_risk: Exact predecessor alone could authorize an obsolete writer to roll the namespace back.

observable_oracle: Wrong-mode and hardlinked leaves miss, replacement refuses insecure leaves, symlink leaves and roots are not followed, and an in-flight root replacement aborts publication.
failure_risk: An attacker-controlled link, mode, or replaced directory could redirect reads/writes outside the private runtime root or overwrite an unverified inode.
notes: Slice 2A descriptor-relative containment and inode checks

observable_oracle: For accounting, maintenance, and publication quota projection, inserting either a valid manifest or checksum-invalid canonical leaf after enumeration but before terminal validation causes insecureDirectory; publication creates no target, and fresh accounting reports two valid manifests or one valid manifest plus the ignored corrupt leaf.
failure_risk: An unchanged directory inode could hide a post-enumeration add or removal, return undercounted accounting, or admit a publication using stale count and byte quota projection.

observable_oracle: Five verified CAS outcomes map to exact manifest outcomes and contribution presence; each ready outcome rejects missing contribution, each terminal outcome rejects a contribution, and a ready artifact rejects the wrong empty contribution.
failure_risk: A caller could detach manifest outcome or graph contribution identity from the exact verified CAS handle and persist an unproven binding.
notes: Slice 2A verified outcome and contribution contract

observable_oracle: A verified clean Git record round-trips exactly; unchanged access advances durably without generation change; codec-decoded records cannot serve as publication proof; storage excludes absolute/display/source bytes; removal yields miss.
failure_risk: Dirty, unverified, or merely decoded state could be republished, raw content or UI paths could leak, an unchanged write could discard access, or round-trip fields could be lost.

observable_oracle: A 32-byte digest round-trips, a 31-byte payload is rejected during decode, and the raw source snapshot exposes no Codable conformance.
failure_risk: Malformed digests or an accidental raw-source persistence surface could corrupt content identity or retain source bytes on disk.

observable_oracle: Empty bytes decode to exact empty UTF-8 text while invalid bytes produce the explicit undecodable failure.
failure_risk: Decode failures could collapse into empty content or unstable parser inputs.

observable_oracle: Repeated identical bytes preserve digest and decode identity, while UTF-8 and BOM UTF-16 bytes for the same text have distinct raw digests and byte counts.
failure_risk: Hashing decoded text could alias byte-distinct source revisions or make identity nondeterministic.

observable_oracle: The immutable envelope retains the exact input Data, known SHA-256 and byte count, shared decoder result, policy, and closed validated-worktree provenance.
failure_risk: Envelope construction could hash or count transformed text, drop source provenance, or decode a different buffer.
notes: Slice 1A evolves the Phase 2 one-buffer envelope to closed source provenance

observable_oracle: Artifact JSON contains only source-derived arrays, excludes identity keys and sentinels, round-trips, ignores injected derived fields, and retains value semantics under copied-array mutation.
failure_risk: Path, root, validation, digest, or mutable derived state could leak into the reusable artifact format.

observable_oracle: The direct builder maps undecodable bytes, empty content, capture-free content, injected oversize and parse failures, and a real line-limit breach to distinct terminal outcomes.
failure_risk: Deterministic negatives could collapse into no symbols, become transient throws, or enter parsing after a terminal source outcome.

observable_oracle: A transient query/configuration error is thrown unchanged and leaves artifact, catalog, and lease namespaces empty.
failure_risk: Operational failures could be persisted as terminal content-addressed negatives and suppress valid retries.

observable_oracle: Sorted JSON uses explicit stable discriminators for no-symbol, decode, oversize, nil-tree, and nil-root outcomes and rejects an unsupported discriminator.
failure_risk: Synthesized or ambiguous enum encoding could make persisted deterministic outcomes schema-unstable or admit binding-local unsupported state.

observable_oracle: Repeated builds and equal raw content under different validation fingerprints produce the same path-free artifact; a Java top-level function stays global in the artifact while two legacy filenames produce their distinct synthetic classes.
failure_risk: Binding identity or filename-sensitive legacy shaping could contaminate the shared artifact graph, or separating terminals could silently remove legacy filename behavior.

observable_oracle: A genuinely empty initial review defers, then authorizes an exact worktree-only committed selection with final-election diagnostics while empty and artifact-bearing finals remain terminal.
failure_risk: Deferred election could broaden root authority, accept no selection, or launder post-discovery artifacts into review.

observable_oracle: Deferred election retains the existing multi-checkout policy and groups each final physical path under its exact elected checkout.
failure_risk: Deferred finalization could collapse a multi-root selection onto ambient repository order or lose checkout/path ownership.

observable_oracle: Nonempty unresolved paths and artifact-shaped auto-codemap candidates remain terminal initial failures and cannot enter deferred election.
failure_risk: Overbroad empty detection could defer rejected inputs and later authorize content that failed the initial review fence.

observable_oracle: A worktree-only logical selection elects the exact physical checkout, emits bounded diagnostics, and refuses canonical same-path rescue after ownership release.
failure_risk: Context Builder could lose owned-root lifetime authority or silently redirect review to canonical content.
notes: Issue #264 Batch 1 exact review-target authorization regression

observable_oracle: An empty canonical Context Builder selection freezes the requested root scope, records deferred review authority, and preserves that authority in the nested snapshot after unrelated roots load.
failure_risk: Empty canonical reviews could remain terminal or silently broaden to a later ambient root.

observable_oracle: Context Builder freezes exact visible linked checkout authority without manufacturing an Agent worktree binding.
failure_risk: Context Builder could lose selected-artifact authority or misclassify a visible root as an Agent projection.

observable_oracle: Classic-first root ordering still elects the CE checkout owning the frozen slice, ignores Classic auto-codemaps, transports the target/revision, rejects final cross-repository selection, and detects root reload.
failure_risk: Context Builder could silently redirect review authority to an unrelated loaded root or accept stale checkout ownership.

observable_oracle: A run-scoped Agent request without context_id promotes an empty selection to an exact worktree-only final authorization, then direct parent selected-scope Git publishes that worktree diff without a selection refresh, while deterministic mutations before and after authorization fail before Oracle invocation.
failure_risk: Implicit Agent routing, a stale parent run snapshot, or revision races could review or publish the wrong committed selection or bypass deferred worktree authority.
notes: Issue #264 Batch 2 routing/fence regression plus fourth-commit selected Git stabilization

observable_oracle: An unbound two-root Agent Context Builder child omits repo_root yet publishes and auto-selects only the CE checkout artifact, then final review uses that patch without Classic leakage or automatic fallback.
failure_risk: Ambient root order could publish an authoritative Classic artifact despite an all-CE frozen selection.

observable_oracle: A bounded queue reports exact overload/cancellation cleanup, and a codemap backlog cannot consume the final queue slot needed by foreground work; both scenarios return to idle.
failure_risk: Queued background work could reject the foreground operation suppressing it, exceed bounds, or leak waiter/owner state after cancellation.
notes: Iteration 2 extends the queue-bound contract with a codemap-specific foreground reserve

observable_oracle: An active codemap read completes, no replacement codemap permit is granted while either exact foreground token remains, and final-token release resumes codemap work in owner-fair FIFO order before reaching idle state.
failure_risk: Codemap fan-out could replace completed reads during foreground work, leak tokens, starve permanently, or violate owner fairness.

observable_oracle: For both interactive-read and content-search waiters, a deliberately aged codemap remains behind queued foreground work and later completes without losing its original queue entry.
failure_risk: Age promotion could let bulk codemap work jump latency-sensitive foreground waiters.
notes: Renames testContentReadSchedulerPromotesAgedWaitersAheadOfNewInteractiveWork and expands the corrected precedence contract to both foreground workload classes

observable_oracle: Scoped foreground activity returns its exact value or error and removes the exact token on success, thrown error, and task cancellation, leaving the limiter idle.
failure_risk: Leaked foreground tokens could suppress codemap work indefinitely after normal or exceptional completion.

observable_oracle: The validated raw API returns exact bytes and fingerprint while telemetry reports one request, one open, exact byte work, no decode, and a caller fingerprint mismatch is rejected.
failure_risk: A probe or reread could double source I/O, or stale caller identity could be accepted into content-addressed work.

observable_oracle: Cancellation at the descriptor read gate throws CancellationError and returns no raw snapshot.
failure_risk: Cancelled raw work could publish partial or unvalidated source bytes.

observable_oracle: Atomic pathname replacement after the first raw chunk is rejected as fingerprintChanged with no snapshot returned.
failure_risk: A mixed or stale buffer could be accepted after a file revision changes during the read.

observable_oracle: Replacing the opened leaf or an intermediate directory with a symlink during the read is rejected by descriptor, pathname, or canonical-containment validation and returns no snapshot.
failure_risk: A symlink retarget could bind attacker-controlled or unrelated bytes to an authorized workspace path.

observable_oracle: Traversal, symlink leaf, and out-of-root targets are rejected as invalid paths; missing and over-limit sources throw their exact errors.
failure_risk: Unsafe, absent, or oversized sources could enter the immutable envelope or cause unbounded memory use.
notes: Phase 2 fail-closed authorization and size contract

observable_oracle: Non-Git and non-loaded workspace roots contribute no visible checkout authority.
failure_risk: Raw workspace paths could manufacture authority without an exact loaded Git root.

observable_oracle: The resolver freezes exact loaded canonical or linked roots, deduplicates them, excludes Agent-bound roots, and never enumerates a sibling checkout.
failure_risk: Source-visible authority could broaden to an Agent projection, sibling worktree, or duplicate root.

observable_oracle: Fake Git observes only controlled capability paths and hardening variables for ordinary and linked layouts; hostile routing/config variables are absent.
failure_risk: Inherited Git routing could redirect cat-file to another repository, object store, index, namespace, or helper.

observable_oracle: Real fake-Git subprocesses preserve the first stdout sentinel, type later overflow, accept exact stderr limit, and terminate stdout/stderr overflow promptly.
failure_risk: Unbounded subprocess pipes could exhaust memory, hang materialization, or erase short-versus-excess semantics.

observable_oracle: Under repository-B routing/object/config environment, A reads its own blob and rejects a B-only blob for both ordinary and linked A capabilities.
failure_risk: Hostile environment state could cross repository authority boundaries and disclose or publish B objects as A.

observable_oracle: A missing promisor object returns local unavailable and an executable ext remote helper marker proves no helper invocation occurred.
failure_risk: Materialization could trigger hidden network access, credential prompts, helpers, or mutable remote bytes.

observable_oracle: Validated-worktree provenance and independent namespace, object-format, count, digest, pipeline, OID, or CAS-handle mismatches cannot mint the opaque association and produce no locator file or publish hook; the valid clean proof publishes once.
failure_risk: An independently constructible locator/key pair could advertise missing, corrupt, or unrelated CAS content.

observable_oracle: Pipeline/key mismatch and identity mismatch fail closed; modified record bytes read corrupt and a proof-bound write atomically repairs them.
failure_risk: A corrupt or cross-pipeline locator could target the wrong artifact key or resist a verified repair.

observable_oracle: Two distinct writers publish concurrently at the count quota without a false integrity collision; maintenance then retains exactly one readable locator.
failure_risk: Quota pressure could misclassify valid concurrent publication as corruption or leave the store above its bound.

observable_oracle: Concurrent stores observe miss or hit without a two-link insecure window, and repeated writers resolve idempotently.
failure_risk: Hard-link publication could create false security failures across actors.

observable_oracle: A 512-entry maintenance pass examines and removes every temporary record without retaining per-record descriptors.
failure_risk: Descriptor lifetime growth could exhaust process file descriptors during bounded maintenance.

observable_oracle: Bounded maintenance enforces count and byte limits and removes corrupt or temporary residue after restart.
failure_risk: An inert locator could grow without bound or retain crash/corruption debris.

observable_oracle: Canonical and linked layouts with one commonDir share a salted path-free namespace; salts remain isolating.
failure_risk: Worktree-local or path-bearing namespace keys could defeat reuse or leak source paths.

observable_oracle: Neither persisted names nor record bytes contain the commonDir path; filenames do not expose raw OIDs.
failure_risk: Locator metadata could leak repository topology or source identity.

observable_oracle: Publication refreshes a stale verified layout when the secure root is replaced before maintenance lock acquisition and still publishes a readable locator.
failure_risk: A stale version directory descriptor could surface ENOENT from maintenance-lock-open or publish against an unreachable root.

observable_oracle: Truncation and atomic replacement after read admission return corrupt or miss rather than model errors.
failure_risk: Concurrent mutation could escape the cache-result model as a thrown decode error.

observable_oracle: Both formats round-trip canonically while bad salt, namespace, OID, format, and trailing bytes fail closed.
failure_risk: Malformed locator identities could alias unrelated artifacts.

observable_oracle: A symlink locator leaf is rejected without reading or modifying its external target.
failure_risk: Locator I/O could follow attacker-controlled cache symlinks.

observable_oracle: Symlinked, wrong-mode, and swapped-to-symlink roots are rejected without traversal.
failure_risk: Root retargeting could move locator I/O outside the private store.

observable_oracle: Wrong-mode, non-regular, multiply linked, and symlinked directory entries fail closed.
failure_risk: Attacker-controlled cache entries could bypass descriptor/path validation.

observable_oracle: Both formats publish only from verified CAS associations, persist as path-free 0600 records, and remain idempotent.
failure_risk: Unproved, partial, or duplicate locator publication could expose inconsistent mappings.

observable_oracle: Attribute and filter configuration mutations across attempts suppress OID eligibility.
failure_risk: A checkout-transform race could publish stale blob identity.

observable_oracle: Cancellation during feature collection never returns OID eligibility and performs at most the bounded retry count.
failure_risk: Cancellation could leak partial eligible state or retry without bound.

observable_oracle: Every configured or attributed checkout transform falls back to exact validated worktree bytes.
failure_risk: A transformed checkout could incorrectly reuse canonical blob bytes.

observable_oracle: Canonical and linked clean files expose the same SHA-1 stage-0 OID; shadow validation counts exact matches and mismatches.
failure_risk: A path-relative or worktree-local identity could prevent reuse or permit a byte-mismatched locator.

observable_oracle: A real cone sparse checkout returns the exact HEAD blob for the included path and sparseAbsent for the physically absent skip-worktree path in one batch.
failure_risk: Synthetic or missing sparse files could be parsed, while included clean blobs could lose reusable identity.

observable_oracle: A real merge conflict surfaces all index stages and porcelain conflict OIDs while requiring worktree bytes.
failure_risk: An arbitrary ours/theirs/base blob could be served as current source.

observable_oracle: Adversarial check-attr output rejects excess records and terminates a finite 16 MiB producer at the 4 MiB capture limit before its completion marker.
failure_risk: Untrusted attributes could force whole-response buffering or unbounded subprocess output before rejection.

observable_oracle: Only exact lowercase sha1 and sha256 values parse canonically.
failure_risk: Normalization could accept ambiguous or injected object-format output.

observable_oracle: Parent gitlinks are unsupported and leaf/component symlinks are security-excluded.
failure_risk: Submodule commits or symlink targets could be parsed as authorized source.

observable_oracle: Index and worktree mutations across both attempts force changed-during-classification fallback.

observable_oracle: Readiness waits return the exact activating workspace generation ticket and later validation rejects the ticket after a newer switch supersedes it.

observable_oracle: A queued root bootstrap grant precedes an older codemap waiter and a newer background request, then all permits drain.
failure_risk: Codemap or background Git work could delay foreground root/search readiness.

observable_oracle: A full bounded queue rejects excess work, cancellation removes the waiter, and duplicate release leaves no active permit.
failure_risk: Admission cancellation or overload could leak scarce Git process capacity.

observable_oracle: Tree, delta, index, and status APIs preserve newline/tab/metacharacter paths while excluding a sibling-prefix root.
failure_risk: Malformed or prefix-ambiguous Git authority could seed the wrong root contents.

observable_oracle: Record/byte overflow and timeout produce typed fallback classifications; cancellation drains the in-flight process permit.
failure_risk: Unbounded Git output could exhaust startup resources instead of failing open to crawl.
notes: Typed cap/timeout/cancellation fallback and permit-balance contract.

observable_oracle: Parser rejects escaping prefixes, sibling prefixes, invalid UTF-8, and unterminated NUL records.
failure_risk: Malformed Git output could be treated as authoritative root inventory.

observable_oracle: Success, failure, and cancellation each invalidate the prior lease once; duplicate completion is idempotent.
failure_risk: Stale authority could survive an app-owned Git mutation or generations could advance twice.

observable_oracle: Real nested-ref creation, HEAD replacement, and ref deletion invalidate each installed lease; final release tears down all sources and polling remains zero.
failure_risk: External Git metadata changes could leave a stale authority lease or introduce steady-state Git polling.
notes: Event-driven recursive and replacement-safe invalidation contract using real filesystem mutations.

observable_oracle: Changing tree, root prefix, ignore policy, or search ABI changes compatibility while namespace/object format remain explicit.
failure_risk: Later reusable snapshots could cross incompatible repository policy or search schemas.
notes: Reusable-snapshot identity contract only; no cache implemented.

observable_oracle: Absent defaults enable both routes; serving still requires observation; explicit false on either key disables serving.
failure_risk: An experimental route could serve by default or bypass its observation prerequisite.

observable_oracle: One opaque correlation spans root-load and root-ready events, the route remains full crawl, and Git command count is exactly zero.
failure_risk: Instrumentation or authority preflight could regress normal non-Git root startup.

observable_oracle: Exact serving control preparation admits the loaded Git root before creating its lease; mismatched and unloaded roots return base_snapshot_unavailable with zero controls; forced-full creates a control without base preparation.
failure_risk: A scoped benchmark control could be armed without reusable parent evidence, producing invalid noReceipt samples, or forced-full comparison could require unnecessary admission.
notes: Worktree startup repair validates base evidence before scoped observation or serving controls.

observable_oracle: A sustained user-authority backlog follows the persistent weighted ring, services codemap/background within bounded gaps, removes a cancelled waiter, and drains all permits.
failure_risk: Foreground Git traffic could starve codemap/background work indefinitely or cancellation could consume a service slot.

observable_oracle: Queued foreground work is strict EDF, root wins an equal-deadline tie, codemap waits behind deadlines, and deterministic expiry/cancellation leave no permits or timers.
failure_risk: Weighted fairness could violate a queued root/first-read deadline or deadline cleanup could leak bounded resources.
notes: Deadline-aware foreground guarantee independent of the ordinary weighted ring.

observable_oracle: A mutation or real metadata event after capture makes conditional install return invalidatedDuringCollection and publishes no scope.
failure_risk: Collection could reinstall and publish stale authority after an overlapping invalidation.
notes: Generation plus synchronous accepted-event watermark closes collection/install races.

observable_oracle: Later retains add resolved sources, a cap failure preserves the prior token/source set, and real external creation, replacement, and deletion invalidate leases.
failure_risk: Silent path truncation, partial retain, or replacement-blind watches could preserve stale external policy authority.
notes: All-or-nothing bounded coverage and additive external-source contract.

observable_oracle: Policy identity includes resolved external contents and every material ancestor/prefix/descendant control, excludes sibling controls, and remains equal when identical external content moves.
failure_risk: Reusable authority could cross incompatible ignore/attribute contents or become machine-path-specific.
notes: Bounded canonical root-neutral ignore/attribute policy identity contract.

observable_oracle: A normal authoritative Git snapshot produces a linked-worktree receipt with the same content identity, exact copied path scope, complete creation witness, matching target authority, and eligible shadow observation.
failure_risk: An unproven or cross-authority receipt could authorize future seeded startup, or exact include-file changes could fall outside the creation interval.
notes: Milestone 8B receipt eligibility and interval proof; serving remains disabled.

observable_oracle: A watcher watermark accepted during provisional capture makes loaded-root admission fail and restores reusable snapshot, alias, byte, repository, and retain-token counts to their exact baselines.
failure_risk: A stale catalog could become production-visible after an ingress race, or failed admission could leak a reusable alias or metadata coverage token into GitService.
notes: P1 exact-ingress admission race and failed-admission cleanup contract.

observable_oracle: Expired, missing, disabled, restart-lost, cross-session, and search-ABI-incompatible evidence produces typed fallback or no cache hit without publication.
failure_risk: Stale or mismatched ephemeral evidence could survive restart, cross sessions, or bypass search ABI compatibility.
notes: Six explicit fail-open receipt/snapshot rejection scenarios.

observable_oracle: Binding JSON round-trips unchanged and contains no receipt ID, correlation ID, snapshot digest, copied path, witness, or initialization-receipt field.
failure_risk: Ephemeral authority evidence, secrets, or copied paths could leak into durable Agent session records.

observable_oracle: Two concurrent same-repository creations serialize safely, independently retain correlation/target receipt identity, share only the immutable base snapshot, and reject cross-binding use.
failure_risk: The first mutation could invalidate the second child's base proof, or a receipt could attach to another session target.
notes: Two-child concurrent isolation and bounded base recapture contract.

observable_oracle: Reusable inventory and matcher paths remain relative and content-address-valid, while a one-entry cache rejects a second admission rather than evicting the actively observed identity and stays below its byte cap.
failure_risk: Absolute target state or mutable generations could enter shared payloads, or bounded eviction could discard active currentness coverage.
notes: Root-neutral payload plus bounded process-local retention without dropping active coverage.

observable_oracle: Seventy unchanged authoritative observations retain one content snapshot, one scope alias, one repository monitor, and one retain token.
failure_risk: Historical lease aliases or monitor tokens could grow without bound and exhaust the 64-retain ceiling.
notes: Replace-before-release preserves active coverage while bounding repeated observations.

observable_oracle: Replacement metadata coverage is installed while discovery coverage remains live, validates only its exact canonical paths/current watermark, then releases obsolete sources deterministically.
failure_risk: A discovery/replacement gap or source-union leak could admit stale external policy authority.
notes: Exact external-policy coverage and current generation are admission prerequisites.

observable_oracle: A source common-directory mutation rejects target capture for a newly created linked-worktree key before Git discovery; generation-fenced capture succeeds afterward and retires its publication when temporary coverage ends.
failure_risk: A target key absent at mutation start could bypass the repository fence, publish mid-creation, or remain falsely current after ephemeral observation ends.
notes: Common-repository fencing is identity-based, not dependent on preexisting authority records.

observable_oracle: Out-of-destination parent/global FSEvents still record MustScanSubDirs, wrapped-ID, user-drop, and kernel-drop invalidation before path filtering.
failure_risk: A stream-wide loss signal reported at a parent path could be filtered out and falsely prove a complete creation interval.
notes: Global witness uncertainty always dominates destination-path relevance.

observable_oracle: Session, startup correlation, logical-root, and ownership-generation replay all fall back, and generation rejection occurs only after the unchanged full crawl establishes a current root epoch.
failure_risk: An ephemeral receipt could cross Agent sessions, startup attempts, logical ownership, or root transitions.
notes: Production requires the predicted owner generation and rechecks root lifetime/catalog generation around evaluation.

observable_oracle: An allowed external worktree and an app-managed creation with include-copy disabled both complete normally but receive no reusable receipt and report typed fallback.
failure_risk: External or incompletely initialized worktrees could enter the reusable receipt path despite lacking bounded app-owned coverage.
notes: Existing full-crawl external behavior remains fail-open while reusable receipts are app-managed only.

observable_oracle: A non-Git directory returns nonGit without executing the injected marker Git binary.
failure_risk: Snapshot observation could add Git subprocess work or alter existing non-Git root behavior.

observable_oracle: A linked worktree created at a different committed tree retains the exact parent snapshot while binding the receipt and evaluator to the target tree.
failure_risk: Same-tree-only eligibility would prevent bounded committed deltas or could confuse parent and target authority.

observable_oracle: A prefix-scoped receipt binds and plans only the corresponding linked-worktree subdirectory and matches its authoritative crawl.
failure_risk: Whole-checkout binding could leak sibling paths or force every subdirectory seed to fallback.

observable_oracle: The bounded manifest exposes assume-unchanged entries and reports repository-wide sparse enablement even when every prefix entry is materialized.
failure_risk: Porcelain may omit hidden index changes or prefix-only inspection may accept an enabled sparse checkout.

observable_oracle: An embedded repository directory survives bounded porcelain validation and recursive filesystem proof rejects its nested .git topology.
failure_risk: A directory-shaped status record could hide a nested repository and be accepted as an ordinary untracked folder.

observable_oracle: Projected membership, IDs, score, tie-break key, ordering, and limits exactly match the authoritative full target index.
failure_risk: A relative-only matcher could diverge for target prefixes or top-K ordering.

observable_oracle: Thirty-one and thirty-two changes both remain exact segmented projections with materialized-search parity, and foreign root prefixes never match or enter results.
failure_risk: Segment or tombstone handling could reorder exact top-K results or leak across root-local graph boundaries.
notes: Enterprise D segmented projection parity; the former 31/32 boundary is a regression point, not a fallback threshold.

observable_oracle: A 20k-entry projection exactly matches the full index while heap peak equals K, comparison work is logarithmically bounded, and one max-key scratch buffer is used.
failure_risk: Per-entry keys or linear insertion could make shadow search O(baseCount*limit) in time or allocations.

observable_oracle: Immediate task cancellation reaches the C scan, returns a cancelled outcome before exhausting the fixture, and joins the detached worker before value completion.
failure_risk: Unstructured projected work could outlive a cancelled caller and record partial parity or consume unbounded background work.

observable_oracle: Concurrent first mismatches produce one comparison, atomically discard the projection, and retained indexes never rerun it.
failure_risk: A copied projection could survive store invalidation or repeatedly execute after known divergence.
notes: Milestone 8C P1 fail-closed shadow isolation repair.

observable_oracle: Inventory and projected-search parity counters aggregate only bounded numeric facts and preserve exact match/mismatch totals.
failure_risk: Shadow diagnostics could expose paths or lose parity failures under concurrency.

observable_oracle: Both controls publish through the ordinary route, perform exactly one crawl, and read the target worktree.
failure_risk: Serving could activate by default or a comparison control could still perform seed work.

observable_oracle: Paused acceptance assigns monotonic watermarks without scheduling and resume schedules the retained FIFO range once.
failure_risk: A capture pause could drop callbacks or start duplicate drains.

observable_oracle: Strict replay publishes exactly through the captured watermark and post-cut callbacks remain queued until handoff.
failure_risk: Replay could omit pre-cut changes or apply post-cut changes twice.

observable_oracle: A collapsed overflow range crossing the replay cut throws before any seeded publication.
failure_risk: Lossy overflow evidence could be mistaken for a complete replay.

observable_oracle: Watcher teardown invalidates the seed identity and cannot synthesize successful watermark progress.
failure_risk: Superseded initialization could publish stale topology.

observable_oracle: A zero receipt journal cut is rejected before stream activation.
failure_risk: An unanchored watcher could leave the receipt-to-attach interval uncovered.

observable_oracle: Every lossy FSEvent flag rejects strict replay with no publication.
failure_risk: A watcher gap could expose an incomplete target root.

observable_oracle: Dirty scan recovery and full-resync requirements reject hidden replay before publication.
failure_risk: Ordinary recovery semantics could accidentally be accepted as seed proof.

observable_oracle: Metadata and app-mutation changes emit path-free invalidation wakeups with accepted generations.
failure_risk: Published seeded roots could miss authority churn or diagnostics could leak paths.

observable_oracle: Current fences do no work, accepted events coalesce to one revalidation, and later instability fails closed.
failure_risk: Metadata churn could cause polling, duplicate Git work, or stale installation.

observable_oracle: Zero and since-now sentinel cuts are invalid while an ordered durable cut proves coverage.
failure_risk: A receipt could claim an interval without a replayable FSEvents cursor.

observable_oracle: Before commit the target is absent; commit exposes ownership, records, projected shard, secure read, and a live post-cut watcher patch together.

observable_oracle: Injected shard failure removes all pending state, releases reservations, and installs one ordinary crawled root.

observable_oracle: Real projected generations append immutable segments across 32 distinct paths, compact segment blocks without materializing the root, preserve retained readers, and maintain exact top-K parity.
failure_risk: Segment compaction could invalidate older generations, lose tombstones, or change deterministic result ordering.
notes: Enterprise D watcher-fenced segmented serving coverage across the former 31/32 cliff.

observable_oracle: The serving projected root index exactly matches materialized results, empty-query order, and limits.
failure_risk: First search could diverge from authoritative target-local path semantics.

observable_oracle: Seed replay and projected counters clamp to bounds, contain no paths, and count a fallback once.

observable_oracle: The synchronous permit accepts an exact current fence and rejects mutation begin before asynchronous invalidation delivery.

observable_oracle: Pair, Engineer, and Design advertise delegation only for top-level external-control sessions; nested roles retain explore delegation only, while Explore advertises no delegation.

failure_risk: Role-policy changes could accidentally alter the direct external MCP tool surface.

observable_oracle: Cancellation before snapshot observation returns the path-free initial_currentness/cancelled failure detail.
failure_risk: Cancellation could collapse into an opaque failure or leak filesystem paths into diagnostics.

observable_oracle: Each currentness checkpoint returns its exact path-free stage with stale_currentness and leaves no reusable snapshot admitted.
failure_risk: A staged admission failure could collapse to base_snapshot_unavailable and erase the branch needed for live diagnosis.
notes: Sixteen coordinator currentness checkpoints, including every streamed catalog suspension, remain distinguishable.

observable_oracle: Wire-level file tools hide the replaced canonical Git marker, expose the linked-worktree marker without physical-path leakage, preserve independent non-Git search/read, and return git_root_unavailable with zero Git commands.
failure_risk: Session worktree projection could leak canonical contents, steal unrelated roots, expose physical paths, or shell out for non-Git codemaps.

observable_oracle: Bare-like and missing or otherwise ambiguous roots fail open to the existing Git preflight.
failure_risk: A conservative local classifier could falsely terminal-cache an unusual Git or inaccessible root as non-Git.
notes: Ambiguous filesystem evidence never short-circuits Git.

observable_oracle: A plain root first proves terminal non-Git without preflight; after real Git initialization and a layout-watcher delta, the cached proof is invalidated, exactly one Git preflight runs, and codemap demand becomes ready.
failure_risk: A cached terminal non-Git classification could survive repository conversion and permanently block codemap admission.
notes: Renamed root/RepoPromptTests.WorkspaceCodemapLocalGitClassificationTests/testGitLayoutWatcherChangeInvalidatesDefiniteNonGitClassification -> root/RepoPromptTests.WorkspaceCodemapLocalGitClassificationTests/testGitLayoutWatcherChangeReprobesAndAdmitsConvertedRepository and consolidated root/RepoPromptTests.WorkspaceFileContextStoreCodemapSeamTests/testRepositoryLayoutInvalidationReprobesCachedTerminalGitSession -> root/RepoPromptTests.WorkspaceCodemapLocalGitClassificationTests/testGitLayoutWatcherChangeReprobesAndAdmitsConvertedRepository; the strengthened row preserves the original two watcher-conversion scenarios, and the duplicate store scenario receives no additional credit.

observable_oracle: A linked-worktree-style .git file always routes to Git preflight.
failure_risk: A gitfile checkout could be misclassified as terminal non-Git.

observable_oracle: A plain directory with no Git control entry in its physical ancestor chain is definitely non-Git; a sibling repository does not cross the root boundary.
failure_risk: Cold non-Git code structure could regress to avoidable Git subprocesses or sibling-root leakage.

observable_oracle: A loaded root whose final filesystem node is a symlink fails open to Git preflight.
failure_risk: Symlink retargeting could make a terminal non-Git classification stale or cross an unseen repository boundary.

observable_oracle: Two demands after a requires-preflight classification each retry a transient repository-changing result.
failure_risk: Transient Git unavailability could be terminally cached and suppress later recovery.
notes: Only definite non-Git is terminally cached.

observable_oracle: Repeated demand classifies once per root epoch, unload clears the cache, and reload reclassifies without invoking Git.
failure_risk: A terminal non-Git result could leak across root lifetimes or repeatedly probe within one epoch.

observable_oracle: A loaded subdirectory beneath an ancestor .git directory routes to Git preflight.
failure_risk: Subdirectory workspace roots could be falsely classified non-Git when the control entry exists above them.

observable_oracle: A catalog-generation change during prepared admission reports failed/prepared_admission_currentness/loaded_root_catalog_stale.
failure_risk: Catalog staleness could be mislabeled as cancellation, watcher drift, or opaque base-snapshot failure.

observable_oracle: Unloading the owner during prepared admission reports failed/prepared_admission_currentness/loaded_root_owner_stale.
failure_risk: Owner replacement or unload could be mislabeled as catalog or watcher drift.

observable_oracle: Typed owner, catalog, and watcher verdicts survive the coordinator prepared-admission fence unchanged.
failure_risk: A Boolean currentness fence could collapse distinct live failure branches.

observable_oracle: Authority invalidation after preparation reports authority_unavailable/prepared_admission_currentness/invalidated_during_collection and admits nothing.
failure_risk: Authority invalidation could be misreported as loaded-root staleness or admission rejection.

observable_oracle: Cancellation at the prepared-admission fence reports failed/prepared_admission_currentness/cancelled and cancels the provisional admission.
failure_risk: Cancellation could be collapsed into stale currentness or admission rejection.

observable_oracle: An unchanged proof serves terminal non-Git with zero Git; creating ancestor .git invalidates it and forces the next demand through Git preflight.
failure_risk: Ancestor repository creation could leave a root permanently cached as non-Git until reload.

observable_oracle: An unchanged intermediate-symlink proof serves with zero Git; retargeting the link invalidates it and forces Git preflight.
failure_risk: A symlink retarget could preserve a stale terminal non-Git cache across a repository-boundary change.

observable_oracle: Admission retains a force-added policy-ignored regular blob as non-searchable, excludes managed-only and untracked paths, preserves the HEAD OID for dirty content, and rejects one missing visible committed path.
failure_risk: Managed-only or missing files could contaminate a reusable baseline, or dirty worktree bytes could replace committed authority.
notes: Catalog-policy projection admission and fail-closed visible-file contract.

observable_oracle: A linked subdirectory plan exactly matches ordinary files and folders while preserving case and Unicode, retaining a visible folder whose only file is ignored, excluding an ignored directory symlink, excluding sibling and .git paths, retaining ignored inventory, and omitting tracked symlink files while preserving visible parents.
failure_risk: Prefix projection, policy revalidation, ignored-entry folder synthesis, or symlink topology could diverge from the ordinary target crawl.
notes: Exact policy-projected linked-worktree equality contract; two added folder-policy scenarios.

observable_oracle: A Git tree containing NFC/NFD-canonically-equivalent but UTF-8-distinct regular paths is rejected with catalogMismatch before reusable admission.
failure_risk: Swift canonical String equality could merge distinct Git paths and admit an ambiguous searchable catalog.

observable_oracle: The receipt-decision buffer retains at most 128 correlations and evicts the oldest terminal aggregate before a nonterminal aggregate.
failure_risk: Unbounded or nondeterministic diagnostic retention could contaminate a one-sample diagnosis or leak memory.

observable_oracle: Four stage writes aggregate under only their correlation while an unrelated correlation remains separate.
failure_risk: Concurrent starts could merge receipt evidence and identify the wrong failed gate.

observable_oracle: A second creation marks the aggregate ambiguous and later terminal writes cannot replace the first terminal stage.
failure_risk: Duplicate attempts could overwrite causal evidence and make an invalid sample appear authoritative.
notes: DEBUG-only ambiguity and terminal immutability contract.

observable_oracle: A correlation export reports schema 6 with path-free receipt evidence, concurrent operation boundaries, derived interactive readiness, planner/lock/passive-tree/marker counters, one terminal decision, bounded=true, contains_paths=false, and no path sentinel.
failure_risk: Diagnostic exports could omit terminal or operation evidence, mix correlations, leak paths, or silently change their internal schema.
notes: DEBUG-only schema-v6 benchmark export contract; renamed testReceiptDecisionSchemaV5ExportIsTerminalBoundedPathFreeAndOperationCorrelated -> testReceiptDecisionSchemaV6ExportIsTerminalBoundedPathFreeAndOperationCorrelated.

observable_oracle: Eviction after arming invalidates the sample and reset clears both receipt decisions and eviction state.
failure_risk: An incomplete or evicted diagnostic sample could be treated as valid across benchmark runs.
notes: DEBUG-only sample integrity and reset contract.

observable_oracle: A natural parent evidence miss records failed recovery with compatibleSnapshotMissing, emits no receipt, and exports no fixture path.
failure_risk: Parent recovery failures could collapse to authorityUnstable and leave the first failed prerequisite unknown.

observable_oracle: Each of five injected receipt gates records its exact path-free failure only for the matching correlation and remains fail-closed; the hook is consumed once.
failure_risk: A hidden gate failure could be mislabeled, affect another start, repeat, or accidentally permit seeded serving.
notes: Five equivalent DEBUG one-shot failure-point scenarios.

observable_oracle: A linked loaded base emits one receipt whose snapshot, repository namespace, common directory, prefix, witness, include-copy, and authority diagnostic identities match.
failure_risk: Linked-base receipt creation could silently diverge from the admitted reusable snapshot or target repository scope.

observable_oracle: The coordinator carries linked-base receipt evidence into one exact binding hint and publishes one seeded child with no noReceipt or full crawl.
failure_risk: Coordinator transport could drop a valid linked-base receipt and force a safe but costly full crawl.
notes: Direct linked-base coordinator contrast for the full service regression.

observable_oracle: A real AgentRun start from a clean loaded linked base consumes the exact token, transports one receipt/hint, installs the child projection and provider binding, and reaches seeded publication without fallback or crawl.
failure_risk: The assembled service route could lose receipt evidence after direct coordinator tests pass, producing live noReceipt fallback.
notes: Full DEBUG AgentRunMCPToolService linked-base benchmark-token regression.

observable_oracle: A cancellation observed while queued behind the common-directory mutation lock creates no worktree or receipt and records one nonambiguous creation terminal.
failure_risk: Pre-lock cancellation could disappear from diagnostics, create a duplicate terminal, or leak a receipt for a mutation that never began.
notes: P1 deterministic queued-lock cancellation receipt-decision repair.

observable_oracle: An injected coordinator error after Git creation leaves the created worktree unbound and terminalizes the single correlated receipt attempt at coordinator stage without ambiguity.
failure_risk: Post-create coordinator failure could leave a pending diagnostic forever or overwrite creation evidence during rethrow.
notes: P1 deterministic post-create coordinator terminalization repair.

observable_oracle: Explicit-tab starts reject both an unavailable frozen source and a captured source-session identity that differs from the live source, reporting the frozen-launch-source failure before provider start.
failure_risk: An absent or identity-mismatched launch capture could silently inherit mutable live state and authorize a child from the wrong source session.

observable_oracle: An explicit-tab launch creates a parentless child; default behavior copies the captured worktree binding and effective path, while inherit_worktree=false leaves bindings absent and uses the logical root.
failure_risk: Explicit-tab routing could incorrectly require a spawn parent, lose its frozen worktree, or ignore the caller opt-out and execute in the wrong checkout.
notes: Explicit-tab parentless inheritance and opt-out regression.

observable_oracle: A parentless explicit-tab child receives both captured logical-root worktree bindings in order and reports two bindings in the start response.
failure_risk: Explicit-tab inheritance could project only the primary root, reorder bindings, or drop a secondary checkout from the child execution context.

observable_oracle: A binding mutation and a source-session teardown injected after capture are each rejected with a changed-before-provider-startup error.
failure_risk: A capture-to-start race could launch the provider after the source tab changed identity or checkout, violating frozen launch authority.

observable_oracle: A source captured with no worktree bindings is revalidated, and a binding that appears before provider start causes the launch to fail as changed.
failure_risk: Treating an empty binding snapshot as no validation requirement could let a newly rebound source launch in an uncaptured checkout.

observable_oracle: The stream replays an event after the explicit cut but before start, and startup returns only after flush plus callback acceptance with the expected watermark.

observable_oracle: An absent strict descendant activates under the existing stable container and counts destination creation events without retaining their paths.

observable_oracle: A destination created immediately after the activation callback barrier fails the second absence check and cannot produce a creation-interval proof.

observable_oracle: Events below and at the cut delivered by final flush are counted, an event above it is excluded, and the exact cut is preserved for replay.
failure_risk: A flush/cut race could omit pre-cut work or consume post-cut work that belongs to the seeded watcher.

observable_oracle: MustScanSubDirs is tolerated only for a proven disjoint sibling; watch-root, destination, and nested destination scopes fail interval proof.
failure_risk: A broad rescan request could be ignored where destination coverage is ambiguous.

observable_oracle: UserDropped and KernelDropped classify as drops while EventIdsWrapped and RootChanged classify as gaps; every case rejects proof.
failure_risk: A genuine journal loss or stable-root replacement signal could authorize seeded serving.

observable_oracle: Ordinary noncontiguous journal IDs prove coverage while a monotonic regression fails closed.
failure_risk: Global journal gaps could be mistaken for loss, or a real regression could be accepted.

observable_oracle: Invalid/sentinel/regressed cuts and every scripted create, start, flush, or barrier failure reject interval proof.
failure_risk: An unavailable durable boundary or incomplete callback drain could be treated as complete coverage.

observable_oracle: 100,001 creation events are accepted without overflow or retained paths, generated in at most 1,024-event batches, and the stream is stopped, invalidated, and released once.
failure_risk: Large monorepo checkout cardinality could trigger correctness fallback or unbounded witness memory growth.

observable_oracle: An existing destination, a non-strict destination, and replacement of the stable root all reject proof.
failure_risk: The witness could watch an unsafe path or survive movement/replacement of its authority root.

observable_oracle: Repeated finish returns identical coverage and performs stop, invalidate, and release exactly once.

observable_oracle: A cancelled finish removes private spill state, and releasing the final lease removes the atomically published artifact and leaves the secure store empty.
failure_risk: Cancellation or reader lifetime bugs could leak root-sized private files or delete evidence while it is leased.

observable_oracle: Manifest membership exactly equals the ordinary crawler, retains deep empty directories and executable metadata, excludes ignored and symlinked paths under policy, and produces the same ordered digest across spill configurations.

observable_oracle: A namespace artifact emitted through the generic spill engine exactly equals the existing schema-1 header, byte-sorted record frames, counts, digest, and footer bytes.

observable_oracle: Mutation and truncation after a descriptor-bound reader opens both fail the next read, leave the reader failed rather than verified, and reject all later reads.
failure_risk: A changed artifact could be mistaken for the immutable evidence stream or a failed descriptor could be resumed.

observable_oracle: After one provisional record is yielded, replacing the manifest path causes the same open descriptor reader to reject the changed retained artifact identity without verifying a footer.

observable_oracle: A provisional source record spills only into an isolated unpublished writer; later source mutation fails validation, removes the staging workspace, publishes no derived lease, and closes the wrapper.
failure_risk: Derived evidence could become publishable before the exact exclusively owned source descriptor reaches authenticated EOF.

observable_oracle: While a transaction is deterministically suspended after ownership acquisition, direct next and a competing transaction are rejected; releasing the gate yields the complete verified artifact.

observable_oracle: A relevant control after 10,001 lazy non-control candidates is found with the same canonical digest as the control-only stream.
failure_risk: Reintroducing an enumerator-cardinality cap would fail enterprise roots before reusable snapshot admission.
notes: Iteration 1 streamed loaded-root Git authority evidence.

observable_oracle: A repository beneath an absolute ancestor named .git still finds its repository-relative descendant control while excluding the repository's own .git directory.
failure_risk: Absolute path-component filtering would silently omit all descendant controls and produce incomplete authority evidence.
notes: Iteration 1 P1 closure: .git filtering is repository-relative.

observable_oracle: Concurrent calls through the actor reader collectively return each record exactly once and reach verified EOF.
failure_risk: Unchecked mutable reader sharing could race descriptor position, digest state, or validation state.
notes: Iteration 1 P1 closure: actor-isolated single-reader serialization.

observable_oracle: 100,000 lazy candidates and tree records complete with exact counts, authenticated EOF, bounded buffers/fan-in/artifact bytes, and final unlink.
failure_risk: Aggregate Data or record-count ceilings would exhaust memory or reject valid large repositories.
notes: Iteration 1 mandatory 100k evidence; no expected-record array or million-file fixture.

observable_oracle: When explicitly enabled, 1,000,000 lazy candidates and records verify exact counts and byte-bounded authenticated cleanup.
failure_risk: A hidden total-record ceiling could remain beyond routine fixture sizes.

observable_oracle: Descriptor corruption, cancellation after spill creation, and aggregate-byte exhaustion fail typed and leave no published or work artifacts.
failure_risk: Partial manifests or leaked spools could be admitted as authority or consume unbounded disk.

observable_oracle: The first pending manifest reserves the full configured cache artifact budget, a second prepare fails closed with an exact rejection counter, commit transfers pending bytes to retained bytes, and revocation returns both to zero.
failure_risk: Ignoring pending or retained spool bytes could let concurrent admissions exceed the process-wide cache disk budget.
notes: Iteration 1 P1 closure: cache-wide retained plus pending artifact-byte budget.

observable_oracle: A stale catalog batch returns the exact stage/cause and leaves zero reusable snapshots, aliases, resident bytes, or artifact bytes.
failure_risk: Missing currentness fences could admit a snapshot from mixed watcher/catalog generations.

observable_oracle: Repeated discard releases all installed and provisional session-worktree ownership before removing the target session/tab.
failure_risk: A failed pre-provider start could remove UI state while leaving a live owner, watcher, or root.

observable_oracle: An abort injected after physical creation and exact nested-root binding construction prevents transition commit and later provider dispatch while retaining distinct worktree and owned-root digests.
failure_risk: A timed-out start could commit a binding after cleanup requested abort, recreating logical ownership behind the harness.

observable_oracle: The bounded owner/path-digest snapshot reports live ownership and watcher counts, remains not drained while another owner shares the root, and reaches exact zero after last release.
failure_risk: Ambiguous aggregate cleanup evidence could authorize deletion of a root still owned or watched by another session.

observable_oracle: Exact assertions keep loaded-root authority diagnostics complete after formerly capped pruned-root records.
failure_risk: Regression could hide pruned-root evidence and admit incomplete reusable snapshot diagnostics.

observable_oracle: Committed control diagnostics remain complete beyond the former retained-record cap.
failure_risk: Regression could truncate control evidence and make compatibility decisions opaque.

observable_oracle: Warm prefix-control cache hits replay canonicalization details needed for authority diagnostics.
failure_risk: Regression could serve cached authority without the diagnostic detail required to prove compatibility.

observable_oracle: A detail request rescans an otherwise-valid cache entry when canonicalization detail is absent.
failure_risk: Regression could permanently lose diagnostic detail after a low-detail cache fill.

observable_oracle: Automatic authority captures coalesce physical prefix scanning and use warm capture hits.
failure_risk: Regression could multiply expensive scans or create inconsistent authority evidence across captures.

observable_oracle: Identical prefix collections coalesce while cancellation remains scoped to the waiting consumer.
failure_risk: Regression could cancel shared work or duplicate prefix scans under load.

observable_oracle: Monitor gaps followed by prefix miss/hit sequences never restore repository metadata coverage incorrectly.
failure_risk: Regression could treat incomplete watcher evidence as authoritative after cache reuse.

observable_oracle: Saturated admission uses a single bounded uncached flight for identical calls.
failure_risk: Regression could spawn duplicate uncached work and exceed resource bounds during snapshot admission.

observable_oracle: Sole waiter cancellation retains flight resources until the slow collector reaches a safe completion point.
failure_risk: Regression could free resources beneath an in-flight collector or leak them indefinitely.

observable_oracle: A cancelled same-key flight remains authoritative until the physical collection completes.
failure_risk: Regression could replace or drop authoritative work while the shared physical scan is still running.

observable_oracle: A cancelled uncached same-key flight remains authoritative until physical completion.
failure_risk: Regression could abandon the only valid authority scan when the first waiter cancels.

observable_oracle: Accepted watermarks invalidate cached footers before actor delivery.
failure_risk: Regression could deliver stale footer evidence after a newer authoritative watermark.

observable_oracle: Corrupt footers and resident budget failures never retain admissions or artifacts.
failure_risk: Regression could admit corrupt authority state or leak durable artifact reservations.

observable_oracle: Monitor-unavailable paths fall back without admission and typed matchers fail closed.
failure_risk: Regression could admit reusable evidence when required file-observation authority is absent.

observable_oracle: Reachability canonicalization rejects invalid UTF-8 in mandatory git-ignore controls.
failure_risk: Regression could canonicalize unreadable ignore controls and admit incompatible policy identity.

observable_oracle: Canonicalization prunes controls below untraversable ignored directories.
failure_risk: Regression could treat unreachable controls as part of the reusable snapshot policy digest.

observable_oracle: Canonicalization retains reincluded and optional-variant controls that remain reachable.
failure_risk: Regression could omit policy controls needed to prove Git authority compatibility.

observable_oracle: The Git-ignore floor rejects secondary negation in both canonical and ordinary traversal.
failure_risk: Regression could let secondary ignore rules masquerade as mandatory authority controls.

observable_oracle: Secondary-only exclusions prune canonical and ordinary traversal consistently.
failure_risk: Regression could diverge policy reachability between diagnostic and production enumeration paths.

observable_oracle: Git negation inside a mandatory chain keeps canonical and ordinary traversal reachable.
failure_risk: Regression could prune reincluded mandatory-policy paths and alter snapshot compatibility.

observable_oracle: Built-in secondary defaults do not masquerade as mandatory Git authority controls.
failure_risk: Regression could pin reusable snapshots to non-authoritative local defaults.

observable_oracle: A loaded repository subdirectory keeps the containing Git-ignore floor as mandatory authority.
failure_risk: Regression could drop parent mandatory controls for subdirectory-loaded roots.

observable_oracle: Loaded repository subdirectories honor scoped Git negation rules.
failure_risk: Regression could mark reincluded subdirectory content unreachable and break policy identity reuse.

observable_oracle: Explicit non-Git and bare policies remain non-Git, while ambiguous Git authority fails closed.
failure_risk: Regression could misclassify non-Git roots or admit ambiguous repository authority.

observable_oracle: Bogus nested Git markers fail closed instead of silently dropping containing authority.
failure_risk: Regression could continue with incomplete repository evidence after malformed nested metadata.

observable_oracle: A valid linked-worktree subdirectory carries the containing repository prefix in authority evidence.
failure_risk: Regression could bind sub-agent roots without the repository prefix needed for diff-seeded startup.

observable_oracle: Mandatory git-ignore symlink, read, and encoding failures fail initial load.
failure_risk: Regression could admit a root before mandatory policy controls are readable and authenticated.

observable_oracle: Mandatory git-ignore refresh failure retains the installed policy rather than replacing it with partial evidence.
failure_risk: Regression could downgrade active policy identity after a failed refresh.

observable_oracle: Nested mandatory git-ignore failure does not cache parent-only rules as complete authority.
failure_risk: Regression could preserve a partial policy identity and later admit incompatible snapshots.

observable_oracle: Reachability canonicalization bounds aggregate retained control memory.
failure_risk: Regression could keep unbounded control data resident during authority construction.

observable_oracle: Directory observation memory uses an aggregate byte budget.
failure_risk: Regression could let observer metadata bypass the resource bounds that protect readiness startup.

observable_oracle: Finalization fences catch omitted control deletion and change after canonicalization.
failure_risk: Regression could publish authority evidence that missed late control mutations.

observable_oracle: Bypass finalization fences reject late control and topology additions.
failure_risk: Regression could admit authority evidence despite late topology mutation in a root.

observable_oracle: Post-artifact finalization fences reject control mutation before authority is accepted.
failure_risk: Regression could retain durable artifacts built from stale policy controls.

observable_oracle: Reachability canonicalization fails before nested Git directory or gitfile controls are omitted.
failure_risk: Regression could skip nested repository controls and publish incomplete authority evidence.

observable_oracle: Canonicalization fails before indexed gitlink controls without a gitfile are omitted.
failure_risk: Regression could accept submodule-like topology without its required Git authority controls.

observable_oracle: The same logical cache key does not reuse a footer across indexed gitlink mutation.
failure_risk: Regression could serve stale authority after repository topology changes under an identical logical root.

observable_oracle: Reachable control add, change, delete, and order changes produce exact digest changes.
failure_risk: Regression could miss policy-control mutations and incorrectly reuse diff-seeded snapshots.

observable_oracle: Reachability canonicalization fails closed for symbolic-link topology in authority controls.
failure_risk: Regression could allow symlink topology to evade mandatory control reachability checks.

observable_oracle: Delta compatibility evaluation covers every receipt field and fails closed on mismatch.
failure_risk: Regression could reuse an incompatible base snapshot for worktree startup.

observable_oracle: Delta compatibility excludes live tree data and freezes missing external representation mismatches.
failure_risk: Regression could compare volatile tree state or silently accept incomplete receipt representation.

observable_oracle: Observed live policy mismatch pairs remain fail-closed during receipt compatibility evaluation.
failure_risk: Regression could mark a policy-mismatched worktree startup as reusable.

observable_oracle: Main-derived ignored controls and a clean linked worktree produce identical policy identity.
failure_risk: Regression could make clean worktrees ineligible for reusable diff-seeded startup.

observable_oracle: Delta compatibility evaluation records are correlation-scoped, bounded, and fail on duplicate evidence.
failure_risk: Regression could merge unrelated benchmark samples or accept duplicate compatibility evidence.

observable_oracle: Canonicalization comparison participates in duplicate and contradiction detection for readiness diagnostics.
failure_risk: Regression could let contradictory canonicalization evidence produce an accepted sample.

observable_oracle: Canonicalization diagnostic summaries remain complete beyond former count caps.
failure_risk: Regression could truncate compatibility evidence needed to evaluate measured startup samples.

observable_oracle: The preparation recorder reports active, completed, and saturating records using a bounded path-free schema.
failure_risk: Regression could leak paths or lose preparation state in debug benchmark exports.

observable_oracle: The preparation registry scopes records, enforces bounds, and evicts only completed records.
failure_risk: Regression could evict active readiness preparations or leak cross-session state.

observable_oracle: Completed preparation registry records expire according to the bounded TTL contract.
failure_risk: Regression could let old preparation evidence contaminate later readiness measurements.

observable_oracle: A client disconnect after control creation revokes the exact owned benchmark control.
failure_risk: Regression could leave stale benchmark controls active after a diagnostics client disconnects.

observable_oracle: Two session-worktree selections under duplicate logical root basenames render with stable disambiguated logical labels and no logical or physical absolute path leakage.
failure_risk: Regression could make worktree-bound full selection replies ambiguous or expose temporary worktree paths to MCP clients.
notes: Oracle review follow-up for projected full selection path display.

observable_oracle: The DEBUG registry retains exact scoped identity without raw paths, duplicate abort is stable, cooperative checks fail typed, capacity fails closed, and post-dispatch gate revocation makes bookkeeping unavailable without requiring a live-start failure.
